elements.sent-2411-006: Such shocks are jumps from $u^-$ to $u^+$, with $u^->u^+$, and propagate, by the Rankine-Hugoniot condition, at a speed \begin{equation}s=\frac{u^-+u^+}{2}.\end{equation}.

elements.sent-2411-008: To study the shocks, we may restrain the equation to an interval $[-L,L]$, $L>0$ and impose suitable Dirichlet conditions on the endpoints: \begin{equation}\left\{\begin{aligned}\label{shocks} \de_tu+u\de_xu =0, u(t,-L) =1, u(t,L) =-1, u(0,x) =u_0(x). \end{aligned}\right.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-009: This system admits infinitely many stationary solutions, which are all of the form \begin{equation}U_\sigma(x)= 1 if x<\sigma, -1 if x>\sigma. \end{equation} Moreover, if the initial datum $u_0$ has bounded variations, then the corresponding solution converges in finite time to one of these stationary solutions.
